Name: neorv32_twd.ctrl.enable
Description: Software-writable enable bit for the TWD module; also drives clkgen_en_o to enable the SoC clock generator used for input sampling and effectively gates major functionality of the peripheral.
Justification: Tampering with ctrl.enable (clearing or preventing it from being set) will stop the watchdog from starting/counting and thereby prevent the WDT from performing system recovery, causing a loss of the module's intended availability/safety function.

Name: neorv32_twd.clkgen_en_o
Description: Output that enables the external clock generator used to produce the sample clocks for SDA/SCL filtering and sampling.
Justification: If clkgen_en_o is prevented from asserting or is driven incorrectly at integration, the prescaler/tick will not be provided and the watchdog counter will not progress, disabling the WDT functionality and preventing the module from performing its recovery/reset role (Denial-of-service on WDT).

Name: neorv32_twd.twd_sda_i
Description: External serial data line input sampled and synchronized to detect bus levels, start/stop conditions and to capture incoming bits from a host/master.
Justification: If the SDA input is driven, held, or otherwise made unavailable/unreliable at integration, the controller cannot correctly receive bytes or detect bus events, preventing normal device operation (DoS). The input is therefore availability-critical.

Name: neorv32_twd.twd_scl_i
Description: Physical SCL input sampled and synchronized to detect edges used for timing sampling, byte framing, and start/stop detection.
Justification: If SCL is not provided or is interfered with at integration, the controller cannot sample bits or detect framing, which prevents transaction progress and results in a denial-of-service for I2C communication.

Name: neorv32_twd.twd_sda_o
Description: Open-drain SDA output driven by the bus engine to transmit data bits and to send ACK/NACK (default released/high).
Justification: This output directly determines bits placed on the I2C bus (ACK/NACK and data). Unauthorized modification of its value or timing corrupts the protocol and transmitted data, so the integrity of twd_sda_o must be preserved.

Name: neorv32_twd.twd_scl_o
Description: Output pin state used to represent controller-driven SCL state (default released/high); used to implement open-drain behavior or indicate controller-driven SCL.
Justification: The output can affect bus timing and transaction sequencing; unexpected modification of twd_scl_o can corrupt sampling/transfer timing and thus corrupt the integrity of exchanged data and protocol sequencing.

Name: neorv32_twd.irq_o
Description: Interrupt line asserted when enabled control flags and FIFO/status conditions indicate an interrupt-worthy event (RX available, RX full, TX empty).
Justification: If irq_o is suppressed, the host may not service the peripheral promptly, leading to idle time and stalled workflows; if asserted spuriously, it can trigger interrupt storms that starve normal processing—both harm overall availability.

Name: neorv32_twd.tx_dummy
Description: Backup of the last transmitted TX byte used as fallback transmission when the TX FIFO is empty and tx_dummy_en is enabled.
Justification: When tx_dummy_en is active the engine may transmit this stored byte instead of FIFO content; misuse or unexpected selection of tx_dummy changes transmitted data (replay of previous byte), impacting integrity of read responses.
